settings.heading: Not a brand. A uniform.
settings.body: I couldn't find a baggy fit in Pakistan that didn't fall apart after three washes. So I made one. That's the whole story — but here's the longer version.

settings.text: Everything on the market was either too tight, too thin, or priced like it was flown in from another planet. Streetwear here got treated like a costume — a logo slapped on a cheap blank. We wanted the opposite: one shirt and one trouser, cut baggy, washed soft, heavy enough to actually own.

We make in small runs out of Faisalabad, ship COD across the country, and keep the range deliberately tiny. No 40-SKU catalogue, no seasonal churn. When a drop sells out, it sells out. That's the point — you're not buying fast fashion, you're buying the fit you'll reach for every week.
Wear it to college, to the court, to the block. Wear it apart or wear the set. Just wear it heavy.
